2605.07121.title: AdaTKG: Adaptive Memory for Temporal Knowledge Graph Reasoning
2605.07121.paper_summary: Temporal knowledge graphs (TKGs) represent time-stamped relational facts and support a wide range of reasoning tasks over evolving events. However, existing methods produce entity representations that are static at the entity level, in that each representation is a function of learned parameters only and retains no trace of the interactions in which the entity has participated. In this paper, we depart from this static view and propose that each entity be modeled as an adaptive process whose representation is refined every time the entity participates in a fact. To this end, we propose AdaTKG, which maintains a per-entity memory that is updated with every observed interaction, with the memory accumulating online and predictions improving as more interactions arrive. Specifically, we instantiate the memory update as a learnable exponential moving average governed by a single shared scalar instead of using learnable parameters for each entity, enabling AdaTKG to handle entities unseen during training. Extensive experiments confirm consistent gains over TKG baselines, demonstrating the effectiveness of adaptive memory. Code is publicly available at: https://github.com/seunghan96/AdaTKG.
2605.07121.paper_summary_zh: 時間知識圖（TKGs）表示帶有時間戳的關係事實，並支持對不斷演變事件進行廣泛的推理任務。<lb/>然而，現有方法產生的實體表示在實體層面上是靜態的，因為每個表示僅是學習參數的函數，並且不保留實體參與過的互動痕跡。<lb/>在本文中，我們擺脫了這種靜態視角，提出將每個實體建模為一個自適應過程，其表示在每次實體參與事實時進行精煉。<lb/>為此，我們提出了AdaTKG，它維護一個每個實體的記憶，該記憶隨著每次觀察到的互動而更新，並且隨著更多互動的到來，記憶在線累積，預測也隨之改善。<lb/>具體而言，我們將記憶更新實例化為由單一共享標量控制的可學習指數移動平均，而不是為每個實體使用可學習參數，這使得AdaTKG能夠處理訓練期間未見過的實體。<lb/>大量實驗證實了相對於TKG基準的一致增益，展示了自適應記憶的有效性。<lb/>代碼可在以下網址公開獲得：https://github.com/seunghan96/AdaTKG。
2605.07121.author: Seunghan Lee et.al.
2605.07121.authors: Seunghan Lee, Jun Seo, Jaehoon Lee, Sungdong Yoo, Minjae Kim, Tae Yoon Lim, Dongwan Kang, Hwanil Choi, SoonYoung Lee, Wonbin Ahn
